news.title: Hybon expects revenue to grow 20-25% in FY27; to invest ₹100 crore in Pilkhuwa facility
news.para: Hybon had recorded revenue of about ₹150 crore in FY26. The company is operating its current manufacturing facility at full capacity and is expanding production through a larger unit in Pilkhuwa, near Hapur.

news.title: Nagpur civic body revives Kachipura eviction drive, 56 commercial units to get 15-day notice
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation is set to resume action against 56 illegal commercial establishments on land leased to Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth. Following a state government order vacating a decade-old stay, notices will be issued, giving occupants 15 days to remove unauthorized structures. The move targets businesses like restaurants and banquet halls, with no current plans to demolish the adjoining Kachipura slum.

news.title: Smartworks to acquire Singapore-based flex workspace provider Workstudio Spaces
news.para: Workstudio Spaces has an operational footprint of about 26,000 sq ft in Singapore. After the proposed acquisition, Smartworks’ Singapore portfolio is expected to expand to four centres with a total footprint of about 76,000 sq ft.

news.title: Patna civic body's tax collections rise after first ARV revision
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation has boosted property tax revenue by increasing the Annual Rental Value by 15%, the first revision in 30 years. This move, effective June 24, 2026, aims to fund infrastructure improvements.  A 5% rebate on lump-sum payments by June 30 for FY 2026-27 is also driving higher collections, with daily receipts surging significantly.

news.title: Launched on May 14, Jharkhand's building regularisation portal gets 4,000 applications
news.para: Jharkhand's ambitious building regularisation drive is off to a slow start, with only 4,000 applications received since its May 14 launch. Confusion over rules, particularly the 25-foot road width requirement, is deterring homeowners. Many are urging the government to extend the July 13 deadline and clarify regulations, while also demanding a change to the plot size limit. Officials promise further examination and clarification.

news.title: Ranchi civic body sets seven-day deadline for fire NOC, Bokaro orders safety audit
news.para: Following a deadly fire in Lucknow, Ranchi and Bokaro authorities are cracking down on fire safety. Property owners in Ranchi have seven days to get Fire No Objection Certificates or face sealing. Bokaro is conducting a comprehensive audit of coaching centres and other establishments, highlighting serious gaps like inadequate exits and a lack of NOCs, with strict action promised for non-compliance.

news.title: Dharwad: Consumer panel raps developers, orders relief to senior citizen in plot registration row
news.para: A senior citizen in Dharwad has won a significant victory against developers who refused to register her residential plot despite receiving substantial payment. The District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ordered the developers to allot an alternative plot and execute the sale deed within a month. They must also pay Rs 50,000 in compensation and Rs 10,000 for litigation costs, ensuring justice for the aggrieved buyer.

news.title: Dubai’s housing market cools after long red-hot five-year rally
news.para: Dubai’s residential property market is cooling after a five-year rally as buyers become more price-sensitive, negotiate harder and shift toward smaller, lower-cost homes amid higher supply and geopolitical uncertainty. While prices remain higher year-on-year, they have softened quarterly, with annual growth slowing sharply from 17–18% in 2024 to around 4–5%.

news.title: Maharashtra orders probe into stamp duty scam estimated to be worth up to ₹20,000 crore
news.para: Maharashtra's Revenue Minister has ordered a high-level probe into a potential Rs 10,000-20,000 crore stamp duty scam. The investigation will scrutinize adjudication proceedings statewide from April 2021 to March 2026, following reports of alleged misuse of powers to reduce assessed stamp duty. A recent Navi Mumbai case involving a clerk processing over 800 documents in ten days, causing a Rs 13.9 crore loss, has triggered this extensive inquiry.

news.title: RBI opens term money market to AIFIs, housing finance companies
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has opened the term money market to All India Financial Institutions and housing finance companies, allowing them to borrow and lend. Prudential borrowing limits for primary dealers have also been significantly increased. These moves aim to deepen market participation and improve liquidity, ultimately strengthening monetary policy transmission by linking short-term and long-term interest rates.

news.title: Haryana's self-certification OC policy under scrutiny after probe
news.para: Haryana's self-certification policy for occupation certificates is under fire after a probe revealed incomplete buildings were granted approvals.  This system, where architects certify compliance without mandatory departmental checks, has raised serious safety concerns and eroded buyer trust.  Show-cause notices have been issued, and a legal challenge is brewing, demanding either policy withdrawal or a complete overhaul with stringent accountability for architects.

news.title: After sealing, owners protest, demand regularisation of PGs in Gurugram
news.para: Protests erupted in Gurugram as PG and guesthouse operators and residents marched against the DTCP's planned sealing and demolition drive. They are demanding a regularization policy, urging the government to legalize establishments by charging fees instead of shutting them down. Concerns were raised about a potential housing crisis for thousands of working professionals and the economic impact on property owners and tenants.

news.title: Telangana RERA bars developer from selling flats, orders ₹15 lakh refund
news.para: Telangana RERA has halted sales and marketing for 'Jai Vasavis ORR Heights' and ordered a developer to refund ₹14.9 lakh with interest. A buyer alleged the project was advertised and sold before obtaining proper RERA registration, with misleading claims about approvals. The authority is initiating penalty proceedings and considering revoking the project's registration due to pre-registration marketing and lack of progress.

news.title: Odisha REAT orders housing project builder to execute a sale agreement for flat booked in 2018
news.para: A major housing project builder in Phulnakhra has been ordered by the Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal to execute a sale agreement for a flat booked in 2018, overturning a refund order. The tribunal deemed the builder's cancellation arbitrary and discriminatory, noting similar buyers received agreements years later. The ruling emphasizes the right to housing as a fundamental right, not just a commercial transaction.

news.title: Mumbai developer booked for duping property investors of ₹1.8 crore
news.para: A Mumbai developer and an associate are facing charges for allegedly defrauding two investors of ₹1.8 crore.  The accused reportedly promised shop allotments in a Bandra redevelopment project but failed to deliver.  One investor paid ₹1.3 crore, while another contributed ₹50 lakh.  When refunds were sought, the money was allegedly not returned, leading to a police complaint and an FIR for cheating.

news.title: New York City freezes rents for one million apartments
news.para: New York City's Rent Guidelines Board has voted to freeze rents on approximately one million regulated apartments for up to two years, a key promise from Mayor Zohran Mamdani. This decision, met with cheers from tenants, aims to provide relief to working families. While tenant advocates celebrated, a landlord representative resigned, alleging the board was unfairly influenced. The move impacts a quarter of the city's residents living in rent-stabilized units.

news.title: Manasum Senior Living to invest ₹800 crore for pan-India expansion
news.para: Manasum Senior Living plans to invest nearly ₹800 crore over the next five years to expand its footprint across India, with plans to launch more than 25 projects and scale up to 10,000 senior living units by 2030. The expansion will span major metro and Tier-II cities, supported by a mix of equity and debt funding.

news.title: NCLT approves Mantra Properties’ resolution plan for stalled project in Worli
news.para: The NCLT Mumbai Bench has approved Pune-based Mantra Properties & Developers’ resolution plan to revive a stalled Worli residential project in Mumbai with an estimated revenue potential of around Rs 2,000 crore.

news.title: Centre approves over 6.18 lakh PMAY-Gramin houses for Uttar Pradesh
news.para: Farmers in Uttar Pradesh will benefit from an extended MSP procurement period for wheat, gram, and lentils until July 8. Additionally, over 6.18 lakh rural houses have been sanctioned under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin's new phase (2024-29). These decisions, aimed at supporting farmers and boosting rural housing, were confirmed by Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan during a review with Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath.

news.title: Karnataka government to simplify e-Swathu norms for gramathana properties
news.para: Rural property owners in Karnataka are set to receive a significant boost as the government plans to simplify rules for e-Swathu records.  This initiative aims to provide ownership documents for approximately 65 lakh inherited properties within gramathana limits lacking formal titles.  The process, expected to be completed within 100 days, prioritizes genuine occupants while safeguarding government lands.  An extension for Form 11B applications is also announced.
